fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Connecticut Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Connecticut - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity connecticut, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to connecticut, speech-language pathologist compact connecticut, connecticut speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Department of Public Health — Speech-Language Pathology

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Connecticut.

license_types.title: Temporary Permit
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship under supervision.

steps.title: Earn a Master's Degree in SLP
steps.description: Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination (test code 5331, score 162).

steps.title: Complete Clinical Fellowship
steps.description: Complete 36 weeks of supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours).

steps.title: Submit Application to DPH
steps.description: Submit application with $200 fee to CT DPH.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

telepractice_text: Connecticut permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. Connecticut is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. A valid Connecticut SLP license is required to treat clients in the state.
provisional_text: Connecticut issues a Temporary Permit for SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Connecticut does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full Connecticut licensure.

faqs.question: How much does a Connecticut SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial fee is $200. Annual renewal is $150. Connecticut requires annual renewal.

faqs.question: Is Connecticut part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: No. Connecticut has not enacted ASLP-IC legislation as of 2026.

faqs.question: Does Connecticut allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Connecticut permits telepractice but requires a valid Connecticut SLP license.

faqs.question: Does Connecticut have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Connecticut. To work in Connecticut, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Connecticut?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Connecticut while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Connecticut does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full Connecticut licensure.
